notes: Install the game, try to start it, it will fail, then execute protontricks 17300 -q d3dcompiler_43. In the run options set Disable Esync [PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1  %command%] . Download the 64 bit Bin64 files for this game  from the steam community and copy them to the game directory. Rename Bin32 to Bin32bak, rename Bin64 to Bin32. Start the game and you're up and running...
title: Crysis

notes:
Requires a couple fixes, and the flashlights act a little strange. Otherwise the game ran perfectly.

Set launch options to disable esync and dx11: PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 %command%

Run "protontricks 17300 -q d3dcompiler_43"

Download 64 bit bins: https://community.pcgamingwiki.com/files/file/257-crysis-64-bit-binaries/
title: Crysis

notes: At first was getting black screen, then a 'crash' back to desktop. Once I got the Bin64 files, I did as instructed: rename Bin32 to Bin32bak (backup), then rename Bin64 as Bin32. Instructions here: https://steamcommunity.com/app/17300/discussions/0/864945401070748061/ Then I ran the protontricks command: protontricks 17300 -q d3dcompiler_43 and even with some errors being reported, it seemed to work. Don't forget to disable esync.
title: Crysis

notes:
The hardwarebreaker for gamers of the "old" times runs very well in DX10 mode, if you have the nvidia 415.22 driver running and esync disabled. Some texture errors (most visibly in the landscape [most likely trees]) are fixed due to the support of vulkan transport feedback, that is buildin in that recent driver package. 
For beginners with Ubuntu: To run the driver installation, you need to blacklist the nouveau driver (google it..), switch to runlevel 3 (sudo init 3) and install the driver as the root user. For more info with the link to the mentioned driver from Nvidia :) : https://www.phoronix.com/scan.php?page=news_item&px=NVIDIA-415.22-Linux-Driver

Running the game @ 25-40 fps in storymode and just for the fun of it @50 - 150 fps on several physic maps (Islandphysics and Physics III) on my machine.
I think the bottleneck on my end would be on the one side the nearly 10 year old cpu (AMD Phenom II X4 955 BE overclocked @ 3.8GHz/RAM (default 1333 MHz)/mainboard (Asus Crosshair III Formula) setup (yea, i'm in need for an update..) and on the other side the CryEngine of that time, that's eating resources like Chrome nowadays, but so far this framerate is not bad at all. Runs worse with Windows.

@ the ladies and dudes of Valve and Khronos: Keep up the work, folks! You are doing an amazing job so far!
title: Crysis

notes:
It ran perfectly after I followed those tricks:
Set PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% as launch options
Run "protontricks 17300 -q d3dcompiler_43" in a terminal.
Then replace the bin32 folder in the original crysis with the bin64 folder from crysis warhead (possibly necessary: rename crysis64.exe to crysis.exe to launch from that). This only needs to be done once to work around the license issue, after which you can replace it with the original binaries again.

title: Crysis

notes:
Only ONE DLL override, d3dcompiler_43 — using a native d3dx10 DLL breaks things for me. The 64-bit Crysis executable from Warhead also causes the game to hang after the very first cutscene; leaving the original binaries in place works best for me.

After installing the d3dcompiler_43 DLL via `env WINEARCH=win64 WINEPREFIX=$HOME/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/17300/pfx winetricks d3dcompiler_43` and setting the launch options to `PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command%`, the game runs fine and performs well using DXVK.
title: Crysis

notes: Works fine with DXVK (DX10 mode) if you apply the mentioned workarounds, frame rate is ok as well even in 4k tested on Plasma Wayland, only intro videos are extremly slow. For me it is silver as I had to download files, otherwise I would have rated this gold as well. 1.) Rename in game folder Bin32 to Bin32Backup 2.) Download https://community.pcgamingwiki.com/files/file/257-crysis-64-bit-binaries/ 3.) Extract to game folder and rename Bin64 to Bin32 4.) Install protontricks 5.) On ArchLinux run in terminal: protontricks 17300 d3dcompiler_43. I didn't need to disable ESYNC!
title: Crysis

notes:
Runs great after tweaks and I was able to run it in DXVK at about the same or slightly better performance than Windows 10. I did have to rename bin64 to bin32 and bin32to bin32.bak and also rename crysis64.exe to crysis.exe in the renamed bin64 file and crysis to crysis64.exe. After that, it would launch and bypass EA DRM server crap through steam itself.  Ran it at 1080p Ultra aka very high or 1440p with generally 60fps+ consistent playthrough except the demanding parts. AA did lower framerate but I used AA x2 or x4 to test the limits. Even no AA is not bad. 
I also did :$ protontricks 17300 -q d3dcompiler_43.  or $:  WINEPREFIX=/path/to/steamapps/compatdata/17300/pfx winetricks d3dcompiler_43 to do a dll override for dx10 to vk, dxvk.  Maybe you can do a dll override with it in winecfg or install d3dcompiler_43 in winetricks or protontricks GUI. 
Launch options: PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1  %command% are necessary.  You can also do -dx9 or PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 %command% if you don't want to run in dx10. Just be wary it is in OpenGL rendering so it will perform a bit worse. Vulkan at least brings performance parity to Windows running crysis in Linux. 

Same can also apply for crysis warhead. Crysis warhead already has 64 bit in bin64 so you can rename that to bin32 and bin32 to bin32.bak and run, maybe rename the crysis64.exe to crysis , do protontricks 17330 -q d3dcompiler_43 and run.
title: Crysis

notes: The only tweak I used is: protontricks 17300 -q d3dcompiler_43 (nothing more). On the fist start game takes a while - it looks like it does not work, but after ~20s it starts. Default settings were high - in the beginning of first level I get 80-90fps. Changed to "very high" - got ~60fps (using my old GTX 770).
title: Crysis

notes:
Using  this launch options works with smooth gameplay and no glitches: 
PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 %command% -force-d3d9 -dxlevel 90 -DX9 -nocrashdialog -noipx
title: Crysis

title: Crysis

notes:
Working great with tweaks at 1080p AA 4x Very High on everything
Tweaks:
1) Download 64 bits version and extract it to the game folder, link: https://community.pcgamingwiki.com/files/file/257-crysis-64-bit-binaries
2) Rename the folders bin32 to bin32.bak and bin64 to bin32
3) Install Protontricks, link: https://github.com/Matoking/protontricks and run in terminal: protontricks 17300 -q d3dcompiler_43 
Notes:
1)There is no need to disable ESYNC
2)Video cut-scenes are slow but these videos don't add anything to the game so just press ESC
3) It's 2019  and graphically the game is still impressive, have fun!
